title: Project build & dependency folders
_comment: Regenerable folders inside your CODE REPOS. The tool groups findings per project (one git repo = one project) so you can pick which repos to clean. 'scan.roots' are searched up to maxDepth; interior dirs named in 'prune' are skipped (this list also excludes tool/cache homes like ~/.npm, ~/.cargo, ~/.local so language caches, global npm, editor extensions and the like are never mistaken for your projects — those are covered by the dev-cache rules instead). kind:project entries only define the regenerable dir NAMES to match (and the method); they are deleted with rm. kind:gitignore enumerates every git-ignored entry via 'git ls-files --others --ignored --directory' per repo and moves them to the Trash (recoverable — git-ignored files can include local .env/config). Junk found outside any git repo is ignored.

categories.name: node_modules folders
categories.desc: Node dependency trees — restore with npm/pnpm/yarn install.

categories.name: JS/web build output
categories.desc: Bundler output (dist, build, out, .next, .nuxt, ...) — rebuilt by your build step.

categories.name: Rust / JVM build output
categories.desc: target/ trees (Cargo, Maven, sbt) — rebuilt on next build.

categories.name: Git-ignored files (all repos)
categories.desc: Everything 'git clean -ndX' would remove in each repo — MAY include local .env/config, so moved to Trash (recoverable).
